description: Phase 5 — In-Process Forge Integration: Create the treb-forge workspace crate that bridges treb's configuration/registry system and foundry's compilation and script execution pipeline. All forge functionality is accessed through Rust crate APIs with no subprocess calls to forge.

userStories.title: treb-forge Crate Scaffold and Foundry Dependency Wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the treb-forge workspace crate scaffolded with its Cargo.toml, module structure, and foundry crate dependencies wired up so that downstream stories can implement functionality in each module.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/treb-forge/ directory created with Cargo.toml and src/lib.rs
Crate added to [workspace.members] and [workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml
Workspace-level foundry dependencies added: forge-script and forge-script-sequence (git, tag v1.5.1)
Crate depends on treb-core, treb-config, foundry-config, foundry-common, forge-script, forge-script-sequence, alloy-primitives, serde, serde_json, thiserror, anyhow, tokio, eyre
Module structure created: lib.rs, compiler.rs, script.rs, artifacts.rs, broadcast.rs, console.rs, version.rs (stub modules with TODO comments)
lib.rs declares all modules and re-exports key public types
Any needed [patch.crates-io] entries added for foundry transitive dependencies
cargo check --workspace passes with zero errors
cargo clippy --workspace passes with zero warnings

userStories.title: In-Process Compilation via ProjectCompiler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to compile Solidity projects in-process via a safe ProjectCompiler wrapper so that compilation never triggers std::process::exit(0) and errors are returned as TrebError::Forge.
acceptanceCriteria: compiler.rs contains CompilationOutput struct with output (ProjectCompileOutput) and project_root (PathBuf) fields
compile_project(config: &Config) -> Result<CompilationOutput> validates has_input_files() before compiling and returns TrebError::Forge if empty
compile_files(config: &Config, files: Vec<PathBuf>) -> Result<CompilationOutput> compiles specific files and validates non-empty input
CompilationOutput provides has_compiler_errors() and artifact_ids() accessor methods
All eyre::Result errors from foundry are converted to TrebError::Forge with messages preserved
Unit test: compile_project with empty project returns TrebError::Forge (not process exit)
cargo check --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass

userStories.title: ScriptArgs Construction from Treb Config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to build forge-script ScriptArgs programmatically from treb's resolved configuration so that script execution parameters are set without clap argument parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: script.rs contains ScriptConfig builder struct with fields: script_path, sig, args, target_contract, sender, rpc_url, chain_id, fork_url, broadcast, slow, debug, dry_run, gas_estimate_multiplier, legacy, non_interactive, etherscan_api_key, verify
ScriptConfig::new(script_path) constructor sets sensible defaults (sig='run()', gas_estimate_multiplier=130)
Builder methods provided for each field returning &mut Self or Self
ScriptConfig::into_script_args(self) -> Result<ScriptArgs> constructs ScriptArgs from Default::default() and sets all fields including EVM args
build_script_config(resolved: &ResolvedConfig, script_path: &str) -> Result<ScriptConfig> extracts RPC URL, sender address, and slow flag from resolved config
Unit test: ScriptConfig::new('script/Deploy.s.sol').into_script_args() produces valid ScriptArgs with correct path and default sig 'run()'
cargo check --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass

userStories.title: Script Execution Pipeline and Console Log Decoding
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to drive the ScriptArgs state machine through the full execution pipeline and decode console.log messages so that script results are available as structured ExecutionResult data.
acceptanceCriteria: script.rs contains ExecutionResult struct with fields: success, logs (Vec<String>), raw_logs (Vec<Log>), gas_used, returned (Bytes), labeled_addresses (HashMap<Address, String>), transactions, traces
execute_script(args: ScriptArgs) -> Result<ExecutionResult> chains preprocess → compile → link → prepare_execution → execute and extracts ScriptResult
Each pipeline stage error is wrapped with context (e.g. 'forge compilation failed: ...', 'forge linking failed: ...')
console.rs contains decode_console_logs(logs: &[Log]) -> Vec<String> that filters for console.log address and decodes all variants
Unrecognized console.log formats are silently skipped (not errors)
cargo check --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass

userStories.title: ContractsByArtifact Wrapper and Artifact Queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an ArtifactIndex wrapper around ContractsByArtifact so that I can look up contracts by name, creation bytecode, or deployed bytecode through a stable API.
acceptanceCriteria: artifacts.rs contains ArtifactIndex struct wrapping ContractsByArtifact
ArtifactIndex::from_compile_output(output: &CompilationOutput) -> Result<Self> builds the index from compilation output
find_by_name, find_by_creation_code, find_by_deployed_code methods return Option<ArtifactMatch>
inner() method provides access to underlying ContractsByArtifact for passing to foundry APIs
ArtifactMatch struct contains artifact_id, name, abi (JsonAbi), has_bytecode, and has_deployed_bytecode fields
Unit test: construct an ArtifactMatch and verify field access
cargo check --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass

userStories.title: Broadcast File Reader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to read historical broadcast files via BroadcastReader so that past deployment data is available as a supplementary data source.
acceptanceCriteria: broadcast.rs contains BroadcastData struct with sequence (ScriptSequence), chain_id (u64), and timestamp (u128) fields
read_latest_broadcast(config, contract_name, chain_id) resolves broadcast directory from config and returns the most recent BroadcastData
read_all_broadcasts(config, contract_name, chain_id) returns Vec<BroadcastData> sorted by timestamp newest first
BroadcastTransaction struct with hash, contract_name, contract_address, function, tx_type fields provides a simplified view of TransactionWithMetadata
BroadcastData::transactions() maps sequence.transactions to Vec<BroadcastTransaction>
Error messages include broadcast directory path and contract name for debugging
cargo check --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass

userStories.title: Forge Version Detection and Integration Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want forge version detection from foundry crate metadata and integration tests against a sample Foundry project fixture so that the compilation and artifact lookup pipeline is validated end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: version.rs contains ForgeVersion struct with version (String) and commit (Option<String>) fields
detect_forge_version() reads version from foundry-config CARGO_PKG_VERSION and returns ForgeVersion
ForgeVersion::display_string() formats as 'forge v1.5.1' or 'forge v1.5.1 (abc1234)' with commit
Integration test fixture at crates/treb-forge/tests/fixtures/sample-project/ with foundry.toml, src/Counter.sol, and script/Deploy.s.sol
Integration test: compile_project against fixture succeeds and returns artifacts including Counter
Integration test: ArtifactIndex::from_compile_output returns index where find_by_name('Counter') matches
Integration test: detect_forge_version() returns a non-empty version string
cargo test --workspace passes (all existing tests plus new ones)
cargo clippy --workspace passes
